# Filesystem-driven Route Generation

Reef's "Next.js-style" filesystem routing: the user writes files under `src/app/`, the build script generates `src/routes.rs` automatically. The generation logic lives as a library function in `cargo-reef`; user projects' `build.rs` is a 3-line file that calls into it.

---

## How user projects integrate

```rust
// build.rs (generated by `cargo reef new`, never edited after)

fn main() {
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=src/app");
    cargo_reef::generate_routes(
        "src/app",                // scan from
        "src/routes.rs",          // write to
    )
    .expect("failed to generate routes from src/app/");
}
```

That's it. Cargo runs `build.rs` on every build. Adding/renaming/deleting a file under `src/app/` triggers regeneration. The generated `src/routes.rs` has a `// @generated` header and gets committed (so fresh clones compile without running build.rs first ‚Äî slight redundancy, big DX win).

---

## Conventions the scanner recognizes

```
src/app/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ mod.rs                              ‚Üí /                            (Home component)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ layout.rs                           ‚Üí root layout for everything
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ about/mod.rs                        ‚Üí /about                       (About)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ pricing/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ mod.rs                          ‚Üí /pricing                     (Pricing)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ _id/                            ‚Üí :id dynamic segment
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ mod.rs                      ‚Üí /pricing/:id                 (PricingShow)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ users/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ mod.rs                          ‚Üí /users                       (Users)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ layout.rs                       ‚Üí sub-layout wrapping /users/* routes
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ _id/
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ mod.rs                      ‚Üí /users/:id                   (UsersShow)
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ edit/mod.rs                 ‚Üí /users/:id/edit              (UsersShowEdit)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ blog/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ __slug/mod.rs                   ‚Üí /blog/*slug catch-all        (BlogSlug)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _group_admin/                       ‚Üí route group: doesn't appear in URL
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ layout.rs                       ‚Üí admin-only layout for grouped routes
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ dashboard/mod.rs                ‚Üí /dashboard                   (Dashboard)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ settings/mod.rs                 ‚Üí /settings                    (Settings)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ components/                         ‚Üí ignored: contains shared UI components, not routes
```

**Rules:**
- `mod.rs` in a directory IS the page component for that directory's URL.
- `layout.rs` in a directory IS a sub-layout wrapping routes in that subtree.
- `_<name>` (single underscore prefix) ‚Üí dynamic path segment `:<name>` (Rust-friendly equivalent of Next's `[id]` since brackets aren't valid module names).
- `__<name>` (double underscore prefix) ‚Üí catch-all `*<name>` (Rust equivalent of Next's `[...slug]`).
- `_group_<name>` (underscore-wrapped prefix) ‚Üí route group, dropped from URL (equivalent to Next's `(group)`).
- `components/` directory at any level ‚Üí ignored by route scanner. UI components live there.
- Files ending in `_test.rs` or under `tests/` ‚Üí ignored.

The component name in each `mod.rs` MUST match the variant name the scanner generates (e.g., `pub fn UsersShowEdit() -> Element`). Mismatch is a build error.

---

## Generated `routes.rs` shape

```rust
// @generated by cargo-reef from src/app/. DO NOT EDIT ‚Äî run `cargo reef build`
// or just any cargo command (build.rs handles it). Edit src/app/ instead.

use dioxus::prelude::*;

pub use crate::app::layout::RootLayout;
pub use crate::app::Home;
pub use crate::app::about::About;
pub use crate::app::pricing::Pricing;
pub use crate::app::pricing::_id::PricingShow;
pub use crate::app::users::Users;
pub use crate::app::users::layout::UsersLayout;
pub use crate::app::users::_id::UsersShow;
pub use crate::app::users::_id::edit::UsersShowEdit;
pub use crate::app::blog::__slug::BlogSlug;
pub use crate::app::_group_admin::layout::AdminLayout;
pub use crate::app::_group_admin::dashboard::Dashboard;
pub use crate::app::_group_admin::settings::Settings;

#[derive(Routable, Clone, PartialEq)]
#[rustfmt::skip]
pub enum Route {
    #[layout(RootLayout)]
        #[route("/")]
        Home {},
        #[route("/about")]
        About {},
        #[route("/pricing")]
        Pricing {},
        #[route("/pricing/:id")]
        PricingShow { id: String },

        #[layout(UsersLayout)]
            #[route("/users")]
            Users {},
            #[route("/users/:id")]
            UsersShow { id: String },
            #[route("/users/:id/edit")]
            UsersShowEdit { id: String },

        #[route("/blog/*slug")]
        BlogSlug { slug: Vec<String> },

        #[layout(AdminLayout)]
            #[route("/dashboard")]
            Dashboard {},
            #[route("/settings")]
            Settings {},
}
```

---

## Scanner algorithm (high level)

```
1. Walk src/app/ depth-first
2. For each directory:
   a. Compute URL prefix from path segments:
      - directory name maps directly (`about` ‚Üí `about`)
      - `_<name>` ‚Üí `:<name>` (extract param name + type)
      - `__<name>` ‚Üí `*<name>` catch-all
      - `_group_<name>` ‚Üí dropped from URL but creates a layout boundary
   b. If `mod.rs` exists: emit a Route variant
   c. If `layout.rs` exists: emit a layout component reference + open a layout block
   d. If `components/` directory: skip
3. Sort routes by URL specificity (longer/more-specific first to avoid match-order issues)
4. Emit the Route enum with proper layout nesting
```

## Param types

Default param type is `String`. Override via attribute in the page component:

```rust
// src/app/users/_id/mod.rs
#[reef::route_param(id: u64)]
pub fn UsersShow(id: u64) -> Element { ... }
```

The scanner reads the attribute, generates `UsersShow { id: u64 }` in the enum. Without the attribute, `id: String`.

## Catch-all params

Catch-all variants take `Vec<String>`:

```rust
#[route("/blog/*slug")]
BlogSlug { slug: Vec<String> }
```

`slug` is the path segments after `/blog/` joined into a vec. Component signature must match: `pub fn BlogSlug(slug: Vec<String>) -> Element`.

## Query params

Not auto-detected from filesystem (no convention available). Declared via attribute:

```rust
#[reef::query_param(color: Option<String>, quantity: Option<i32>)]
pub fn Product(color: Option<String>, quantity: Option<i32>) -> Element { ... }
```

The scanner picks this up, generates the route with appended query syntax: `#[route("/product?color&quantity")]`.

## Build hooks

The `cargo_reef::generate_routes` function:

1. Validates that every `mod.rs` under `src/app/` declares a `pub fn` with the expected name
2. Validates layout components have `Outlet::<Route> {}` somewhere in their body (heuristic: parses for the literal text)
3. Writes `routes.rs` only if its contents differ from existing ‚Äî avoids spurious recompiles
4. Emits `cargo:rerun-if-changed=src/app` so additions trigger regen

## Errors point at offending files

Bad outputs:

```
error: file `src/app/users/_id/mod.rs` declares a page but its component is named `UserShow`,
       expected `UsersShow` (matching the directory's URL pattern).
       Either rename the function to `UsersShow`, or add `#[reef::component_name = "UserShow"]`.

error: directory `src/app/_id` is a dynamic segment but no parent specifies a name ‚Äî
       dynamic segments need a parent (e.g., `src/app/users/_id` for `/users/:id`).
```

## Validation runs at build time, not compile time

The `routes.rs` we generate compiles fine ‚Äî Rust's compiler catches "function named X doesn't exist" errors. But friendly-error reporting (point at the .rs file, suggest the fix) lives in the scanner, not in rustc's errors. Worth the extra effort because routing is the place users get hung up most.

## v0.1 status: not built

For v0.1 of the reef-template, users hand-write `src/routes.rs`. Documentation in the file itself notes the v0.5 plan. When v0.5 ships, `build.rs` becomes a real script and `routes.rs` becomes generated; existing apps adopt by running `cargo reef upgrade`.

## Why build.rs over proc macro

Documented in detail in `reef-template/build.rs`. Short version:

- build scripts have first-class filesystem watching via `cargo:rerun-if-changed`
- proc macros don't reliably re-run when external files change
- generated file on disk is debuggable; macro-expanded code is opaque
- same reason `prost`, `cxx`, etc. use build scripts for codegen
